evidence.scientific_clarity: Left-to-right pipeline with solid data-flow and dashed control/feedback arrows, group boxes for Retrieval and Generation — brief fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none set; PDF uses Type3 outlined glyphs and SVG has 0 <text> elements (73 path glyphs) — text is not selectable/editable. Font is DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default), not a publication choice.
evidence.palette: Blue/yellow/green/purple categorical palette; no red/green directional misuse. Blue (#2266aa) and green (#227722) edge colors may be confused by deuteranopes, but shapes and labels disambiguate.
evidence.layout_density: 10×5 inch canvas with 6 nodes well-spaced, legend in lower-left, title at top — no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately matches figure content; legend covers all visual encodings. Minor issue: input and output nodes share the same legend entry ('Input/Output') despite different edge colors, and no arrow legend distinguishes solid vs dashed visually in the patch legend (uses Line2D correctly).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector (paths/patches are vector) but fonts are Type3 outlined glyphs — text is not selectable. Script omits matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (30 KB), figure.png (70 KB, 1372×735 px), figure.svg (56 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial. data.json absent from work dir but script ran successfully.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all nodes and arrow semantics and references data.json provenance, but offers no quantitative or specific architectural finding — acceptable for a diagram caption but could note e.g. number of stages or the feedback loop's role.

overall_strengths: Brief fully implemented: solid/dashed arrow distinction, rounded-rectangle group boxes, left-to-right data flow, and cylinder datastore shape all present.
All required output files exist and are non-trivial; layout is clean with no overlapping labels and a complete legend.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype=none: text is baked into outlines in both PDF and SVG, making it non-selectable and non-editable by journal production.
DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) used throughout — a publication-quality font (e.g. Helvetica, Libertine, or a serif matching the paper) should be specified explicitly.
